content_en:
In 6 Hijrah fourteen hundred Companions set out for Makkah with the Prophet 38 to perform ‘Umrah. They were stopped at Hudaibiyah. Finally a treaty was concluded between the Prophet 3% and the Quraish of Makkah, which came to be known as the Treaty of Hudaibiyah. ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin was one of these pure-hearted

Companions, who are described in the Noble Qur’an in these words of Sarah Al-Fath:

“Indeed Allah was pleased with the believers when they gave the Bai‘ah (pledge) to you (O Muhammad 3) under the tree: He knew what was in their hearts, and He sent down As-Sakinah (calmness and tranquillity) upon them, and He rewarded them with a near victory.” (48:18)

During the caliphate Abu Bakr Siddiq 4 subversion and perfidy had reached their peak. Instead of feeling upset and panicking, he displayed determination and courage and declared Jihdd against the apostates. In order to subdue these traitors he sent armies of Mujahideen in various directions. The very famous and cunning boy of the Banu Asad tribe, Taleehah bin Khawaled Asadi declared that he was a prophet. Of the Arab tribes, Banu Tai and Banu Farazah had united with the Banu Asad. The Prophet 3 had appointed Dharar bin Azwar -$, a brave and heroic warrior of the Banu Asad to suppress and overpower him. He showed his experience and valor in defeating Taleehah Asadi and his army. Taleehah, in an effort to save his life escaped to a place called Bazakhah and took up residence there.

Abu Bakr Siddiq 4 ordered the great Muslim General Khalid bin Waleed 4 to suppress Taleehah and he set off for Bazakhah. Khalid @ sent ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin and Thabit bin Aqram Uys 4 2, first to find the main route to the battlefield and check its condition. It so happened that Taleehah and his brother Salamah were riding in the area. Taking advantage of the opportunity they attacked ‘Ukashah

This incident had taken place before the Muslim army had reached the battlefield. When Khalid bin Waleed .# saw the bodies of these two martyrs he was close to tears; he buried them in the same bloodsoaked clothes and turned in fury to challenge the enemy. Taleehah’s army faced a disgraceful defeat in the battlefield and he himself took to his heels.

He finally managed to find refuge in Syria. There he repented and embraced Islam again. From Syria he decided to proceed to Makkah for the purpose of performing ‘Umrah. While he was passing AlMadinah, a person who reported his intentions to the Caliph Abu Bakr Siddig spotted him. But caliph Abu Bakr Siddiq 8 said he could not be prevented from performing ‘Umrah since he had come back into the fold of Islam.

During the Caliphate of ‘Umar Farooq -# he wished to swear allegiance to him in person, but the Caliph was very angry with him. He told him:

“You have tortured Muslims.” Taleehah pleaded his case and said:

“Allah 3% accepts the true repentance of a sinner; | wish to swear allegiance to you in person and find redemption for my past sins. Please be kind enough to give me this opportunity.”

‘Umar Faroog -# finally agreed and his future was blessed because he had returned to the fold of Islam. If Allah wishes to Blessed a person with True Faith no one else can stand in his way or deprive him.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

‘Abdullah bin Jahash 2 The Prophet # said:

“O ‘Abdullah! You should be delighted because a grand palace has been made for you in Paradise.”

‘Abdullah bin Jahash 2

‘Abdullah bin Jahash @ is one of the most brilliant stars in the firmament of Islam and a torch-bearer of the new religion. He was the son of Umaima bint ‘Abdul Mutallib, the aunt of the Prophet 38. His sister was Umm-ul-Mo'mincen Zainab bint Jahash \s 4) 2). He was a man of medium height with a sturdy well-built body and his thick hair framed a beautiful face. He had supreme patience and could bear up with hunger and thirst with great fortitude. He was one of them whom accept Islam first; the Prophet # had just sat in Dar-Arqam when ‘Abdullah bin Jahash came in and swore allegiance to Islam.

According to one tradition, he was appointed as a General of the first Islamic troop. He also had the good fortune and distinction of being the first great Companion to receive the flag of Islam from the hands of the Prophet 38 himself and lead a band of the Mujahideen. He loved martyrdom more than this borrowed lifetime. In the battlefield of Uhud, he offered such a unique supplication, which moved the listeners deeply. This supplication was honoured. His Ijtehad was proved by Allah 3% regarding distribution of one-fifth spoils of war in the Noble Qur’an. He was martyred in the Battle of Uhud and had the distinction of finding tranquillity and repose in the same grave as the Leader of the Martyrs, Amir Hamzah -%, the dearly beloved uncle of the Prophet 38.

When the Prophet % saw his Companions being persecuted and tortured by the Quraish, he gave them permission to migrate to AlMadinah. ‘Abdullah bin Jahash + decided to migrate and take his

whole family with him. It was not a difficult decision for him to leave behind a settled life in his hometown and resettle in a strange new place, since it was for the sake of Allah. He had earlier migrated to Ethiopia as well with the first band of migrants, so really speaking it was not a unique experience. But this time round migration was ona larger scale, since all his family was to traveling together.

When the family left Makkah for Al-Madinah the houses wore a forlorn deserted look. One day Abu Jahl and Shaibah visited these deserted grand palaces. They were surprised to see that the doors and windows had been left wide open and they were banging in the strong winds. It seemed as if no one had ever lived in them before. Abu Jahl took over the magnificent palace of ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 4 and started living there. When ‘Abdullah 4 heard this news he felt sorrowful that a disbeliever has occupying it. He asked the Prophet 38:

“O Prophet! What has happened? The worst man in the creation of Allah 3% has occupied my house.”

When the Prophet 38 saw his distress he consoled him, saying:

“Would you not like it that Allah make a beautiful and grand palace for you in Paradise?”

“Why not O Prophet; if this happens then what greater happiness could I want?”

Then the Prophet 3 said:

“O ‘Abdullah! You should be delighted because a grand palace has been made for you in Paradise.”

His pleasure and gratification knew no bounds. The news of a palace in Paradise, made one must be the very pinnacle and peak of perfection. Any Muslim could aspire such a thing to catch this honour. It is only natural that his heart started to dance with happiness. The Companions of the Prophet 8 were those fortunate people, who have sacrificed all for him and Islam. They heard such tidings of honour from him personally. The azure dome of the heavens could never have seen a more noble, honorable and self-respecting band of people

than the Companions of the Prophet 38. These are hallowed and sacred souls who have found honourable mention in the Book of Allah Himself. The minor regret that ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 4 may have felt at the worst of men takeover his house and wealth. He was instantly changed into rapture on this thought. Doubtless the life of this world and all its trappings are transient and temporary. It is the life Hereafter that is Eternal and Everlasting and which was belongs rightfully to the respected Companions. They were given the glad tidings of success not only in this world, but also in the life after death.

“Today I am appointing a man as your leader, who is capable of bearing up with hunger and thirst better than any of you.”

As he said this he handed the troop’s flag to ‘Abdullah bin Jahash #5. He also gave hima letter and told him:

“Open this letter and read it after you have travelled for two days and follow the instructions given in it.”

The leader, ‘Abdullah bin Jahash -4, opened the letter after two days as instructed by the Prophet 38. The following was the text of the letter:

“When you read this letter do not stop, but keep on advancing until you reach Nakhlah, which is between Taif and Makkah. Set up camp at Nakhlah, and keep a very close watch on the Quraish. If you detect any unusual or dangerous movements inform me immediately.”

When ‘Abdullah bin Jahash -¢ read the instructions of the Prophet 3, and told his companions:

“{ have been ordered by the Prophet 3 to set up camp at Nakhlah and keep a watch on the movements of the Quraish. It is quite possible that our lives may be at risk, so those of you

who wish to attain martyrdom please accompany me. There may be some of you who value their life more than their mission; in case if they wish to go back, they can do so. No objections will be raised.”
content_id:
Pada tahun 6 Hijriah, empat belas ratus Sahabat berangkat ke Makkah bersama Nabi ﷺ untuk menunaikan 'Umrah. Mereka dihentikan di Hudaibiyah. Akhirnya sebuah perjanjian disepakati antara Nabi ﷺ dan kaum Quraisy Makkah, yang kemudian dikenal sebagai Perjanjian Hudaibiyah. 'Ukashah bin Mohsin adalah salah satu dari para

Sahabat yang berhati mulia ini, yang digambarkan dalam Al-Qur'an Al-Karim dengan firman Allah dalam Surah Al-Fath:

"Sungguh, Allah telah ridha terhadap orang-orang mukmin ketika mereka berjanji setia (Bai'ah) kepadamu (wahai Muhammad ﷺ) di bawah pohon: Dia mengetahui apa yang ada dalam hati mereka, lalu Dia menurunkan As-Sakinah (ketenangan dan ketenteraman) atas mereka, dan memberi balasan kepada mereka dengan kemenangan yang dekat." (48:18)

Selama kekhalifahan Abu Bakr Siddiq رضي الله عنه, pemberontakan dan pengkhianatan telah mencapai puncaknya. Alih-alih merasa gelisah dan panik, beliau menunjukkan keteguhan dan keberanian serta menyatakan jihad melawan para murtad. Untuk menundukkan para pengkhianat ini, beliau mengirim pasukan Mujahidin ke berbagai arah. Anak muda yang sangat terkenal dan licik dari suku Banu Asad, Taleehah bin Khawaled Asadi menyatakan dirinya sebagai nabi. Dari suku-suku Arab, Banu Tai dan Banu Farazah telah bersekutu dengan Banu Asad. Nabi ﷺ telah menunjuk Dharar bin Azwar رضي الله عنه, seorang pejuang pemberani dan gagah dari Banu Asad untuk menumpas dan mengalahkannya. Beliau menunjukkan pengalaman dan keberaniannya dalam mengalahkan Taleehah Asadi dan pasukannya. Taleehah, dalam upaya menyelamatkan nyawanya, melarikan diri ke sebuah tempat bernama Bazakhah dan menetap di sana.

Abu Bakr Siddiq رضي الله عنه memerintahkan panglima besar Muslim Khalid bin Waleed رضي الله عنه untuk menumpas Taleehah, dan beliau berangkat menuju Bazakhah. Khalid رضي الله عنه mengirim 'Ukashah bin Mohsin dan Thabit bin Aqram رضي الله عنهما terlebih dahulu untuk menemukan rute utama menuju medan pertempuran dan memeriksa kondisinya. Kebetulan Taleehah dan saudaranya Salamah sedang berkuda di daerah tersebut. Memanfaatkan kesempatan itu, mereka menyerang 'Ukashah.

Peristiwa ini terjadi sebelum pasukan Muslim mencapai medan pertempuran. Ketika Khalid bin Waleed رضي الله عنه melihat jasad kedua syuhada ini, beliau hampir meneteskan air mata; beliau memakamkan mereka dalam pakaian yang sama yang berlumuran darah dan berbalik dengan penuh amarah untuk menantang musuh. Pasukan Taleehah menghadapi kekalahan yang memalukan di medan pertempuran dan dia sendiri melarikan diri.

Akhirnya dia berhasil menemukan perlindungan di Syam. Di sana dia bertaubat dan memeluk Islam kembali. Dari Syam dia memutuskan untuk pergi ke Makkah untuk menunaikan 'Umrah. Ketika dia melewati Al-Madinah, seseorang yang melaporkan niatnya kepada Khalifah Abu Bakr Siddiq melihatnya. Namun Khalifah Abu Bakr Siddiq رضي الله عنه berkata bahwa dia tidak bisa dihalangi untuk menunaikan 'Umrah karena dia telah kembali ke pangkuan Islam.

Selama kekhalifahan 'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه, dia ingin berbaiat kepadanya secara langsung, tetapi Khalifah sangat marah kepadanya. Beliau berkata kepadanya:

"Kamu telah menyiksa kaum Muslimin." Taleehah membela dirinya dan berkata:

"Allah ﷻ menerima taubat yang tulus dari seorang pendosa; saya ingin berbaiat kepadamu secara langsung dan menemukan penebusan atas dosa-dosa masa laluku. Mohon kiranya berkenan memberiku kesempatan ini."

'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه akhirnya setuju dan masa depannya diberkahi karena dia telah kembali ke pangkuan Islam. Jika Allah berkehendak memberkahi seseorang dengan iman yang benar, tidak ada seorang pun yang dapat menghalangi jalannya atau merampasnya darinya.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha dan diridhai!" (89:28)

'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Wahai 'Abdullah! Hendaklah engkau bergembira karena sebuah istana yang megah telah dibangun untukmu di Surga."

'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه

'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه adalah salah satu bintang paling cemerlang di cakrawala Islam dan pembawa obor agama baru ini. Beliau adalah putra Umaima binti 'Abdul Muthallib, bibi Nabi ﷺ. Saudara perempuannya adalah Ummul Mukminin Zainab binti Jahash رضي الله عنها. Beliau adalah seorang pria bertubuh sedang dengan badan yang kokoh dan kekar, rambut tebalnya membingkai wajah yang tampan. Beliau memiliki kesabaran yang tinggi dan mampu menanggung lapar dan haus dengan ketabahan yang luar biasa. Beliau adalah salah satu orang yang pertama kali masuk Islam; Nabi ﷺ baru saja duduk di Dar Al-Arqam ketika 'Abdullah bin Jahash datang dan berbaiat masuk Islam.

Menurut satu riwayat, beliau ditunjuk sebagai panglima pasukan Islam yang pertama. Beliau juga mendapat keberuntungan dan kehormatan menjadi Sahabat agung pertama yang menerima bendera Islam dari tangan Nabi ﷺ sendiri dan memimpin sekelompok Mujahidin. Beliau mencintai syahadah lebih dari kehidupan sementara ini. Di medan pertempuran Uhud, beliau memanjatkan doa yang begitu unik, yang mengharukan para pendengarnya. Doa ini dikabulkan. Ijtihadnya dibuktikan oleh Allah ﷻ mengenai pembagian seperlima rampasan perang dalam Al-Qur'an Al-Karim. Beliau gugur sebagai syahid dalam Perang Uhud dan mendapat kehormatan menemukan ketenangan dan kedamaian di makam yang sama dengan Pemimpin Para Syuhada, Amir Hamzah رضي الله عنه, paman tercinta Nabi ﷺ.

Ketika Nabi ﷺ melihat para Sahabatnya dianiaya dan disiksa oleh kaum Quraisy, beliau memberi mereka izin untuk berhijrah ke Al-Madinah. 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه memutuskan untuk berhijrah dan membawa seluruh

keluarganya bersamanya. Bukanlah keputusan yang sulit baginya untuk meninggalkan kehidupan yang mapan di kampung halamannya dan menetap kembali di tempat asing yang baru, karena semua itu demi Allah. Beliau sebelumnya juga telah berhijrah ke Ethiopia bersama rombongan pertama para muhajirin, jadi sebenarnya ini bukanlah pengalaman yang baru. Namun kali ini hijrah dalam skala yang lebih besar, karena seluruh keluarganya bepergian bersama.

Ketika keluarga itu meninggalkan Makkah menuju Al-Madinah, rumah-rumah tampak sepi dan terbengkalai. Suatu hari Abu Jahl dan Shaibah mengunjungi istana-istana megah yang terbengkalai ini. Mereka terkejut melihat pintu-pintu dan jendela-jendela dibiarkan terbuka lebar dan berderak-derak tertiup angin kencang. Seolah-olah tidak pernah ada orang yang tinggal di dalamnya. Abu Jahl mengambil alih istana megah 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه dan mulai tinggal di sana. Ketika 'Abdullah رضي الله عنه mendengar berita ini, beliau merasa sedih bahwa seorang kafir menempatinya. Beliau bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ:

"Wahai Nabi! Apa yang terjadi? Manusia terburuk dalam ciptaan Allah ﷻ telah menempati rumahku."

Ketika Nabi ﷺ melihat kesedihannya, beliau menghiburnya dengan bersabda:

"Tidakkah engkau suka jika Allah membuatkan sebuah istana yang indah dan megah untukmu di Surga?"

"Tentu saja wahai Nabi; jika itu terjadi maka kebahagiaan apa lagi yang lebih besar yang bisa kuinginkan?"

Kemudian Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Wahai 'Abdullah! Hendaklah engkau bergembira karena sebuah istana yang megah telah dibangun untukmu di Surga."

Kegembiraan dan rasa syukurnya tidak terbatas. Kabar tentang sebuah istana di Surga, pastilah merupakan puncak dan kesempurnaan tertinggi. Setiap Muslim bisa beraspirasi untuk meraih kehormatan seperti itu. Sungguh wajar jika hatinya menari-nari kegembiraan. Para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ adalah orang-orang yang beruntung, yang telah mengorbankan segalanya untuk beliau dan Islam. Mereka mendengar kabar kehormatan seperti itu langsung dari beliau. Langit biru yang membentang tidak pernah melihat sekelompok manusia yang lebih mulia, terhormat, dan bermartabat

daripada para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ. Mereka adalah jiwa-jiwa suci dan mulia yang telah mendapat sebutan terhormat dalam Kitab Allah sendiri. Sedikit rasa penyesalan yang mungkin dirasakan 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه atas orang terburuk yang mengambil alih rumah dan hartanya. Perasaan itu langsung berubah menjadi kegembiraan atas pikiran ini. Tidak diragukan lagi, kehidupan dunia ini dan segala perangkapnya bersifat fana dan sementara. Kehidupan akhiratlah yang Abadi dan Kekal yang menjadi hak para Sahabat yang terhormat. Mereka diberi kabar gembira tentang keberhasilan tidak hanya di dunia ini, tetapi juga di kehidupan setelah kematian.

"Hari ini aku menunjuk seorang pria sebagai pemimpin kalian, yang mampu menanggung lapar dan haus lebih baik dari siapa pun di antara kalian."

Seraya berkata demikian, beliau menyerahkan bendera pasukan kepada 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه. Beliau juga memberinya sebuah surat dan berpesan kepadanya:

"Buka surat ini dan bacalah setelah engkau berjalan selama dua hari dan ikuti petunjuk yang ada di dalamnya."

Pemimpin pasukan, 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه, membuka surat itu setelah dua hari sebagaimana yang diperintahkan oleh Nabi ﷺ. Berikut ini adalah isi surat tersebut:

"Ketika engkau membaca surat ini, jangan berhenti, tetapi teruslah maju hingga engkau mencapai Nakhlah, yang terletak di antara Taif dan Makkah. Dirikan kemah di Nakhlah, dan awasi pergerakan kaum Quraisy dengan sangat ketat. Jika engkau mendeteksi gerakan yang tidak biasa atau berbahaya, segera beritahu aku."

Ketika 'Abdullah bin Jahash رضي الله عنه membaca perintah Nabi ﷺ, dan memberitahu para sahabatnya:

"Aku telah diperintahkan oleh Nabi ﷺ untuk mendirikan kemah di Nakhlah dan mengawasi pergerakan kaum Quraisy. Sangat mungkin nyawa kita akan terancam, maka di antara kalian yang

yang ingin meraih syahadah (mati syahid), silakan ikut bersamaku. Mungkin ada di antara kalian yang lebih menghargai nyawa mereka daripada misi mereka; jika mereka ingin kembali, mereka boleh melakukannya. Tidak ada keberatan yang akan diajukan."
content_ar: In 6 Hijrah fourteen hundred الصحابة set out for مكة المكرمة with النبي ﷺ 38 to perform ‘العمرة. They were stopped at Hudaibiyah. وأخيراً a treaty was concluded between النبي ﷺ 3% and the Quraish of مكة المكرمة, which came to be known as the Treaty of Hudaibiyah. ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin was one of these pure-hearted الصحابة, who are described in the Noble Qur’an in these words of Sarah Al-Fath: “Indeed الله was pleased with the المؤمنون عندما they gave the Bai‘ah (pledge) to you (O محمد ﷺ 3) under the tree: He knew what was in their القلوب, and He sent down As-Sakinah (calmness and tranquillity) upon them, and He rewarded them with a near النصر.” (48:18) خلال the الخلافة أبو بكر Siddiq 4 subversion and perfidy had reached their peak. Instead of feeling upset and panicking, he displayed determination and الشجاعة and declared Jihdd against the apostates. من أجل subdue these traitors he sent armies of المجاهدين in various directions. The very famous and cunning boy of the Banu Asad tribe, Taleehah bin Khawaled Asadi declared that he was a النبي. Of the Arab tribes, Banu Tai and Banu Farazah had united with the Banu Asad. النبي ﷺ 3 had appointed Dharar bin Azwar -$, a brave and heroic warrior of the Banu Asad to suppress and overpower him. He showed his experience and valor in الهزيمةing Taleehah Asadi and his الجيش. Taleehah, in an effort to save his الحياة escaped to a place called Bazakhah and took up residence there. أبو بكر Siddiq 4 ordered the great مسلم General Khalid bin Waleed 4 to suppress Taleehah and he set off for Bazakhah. Khalid @ sent ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin and Thabit bin Aqram Uys 4 2, أولاً to find the الرئيسي route to the المعركةfield and check its condition. It لذا happened that Taleehah and his الأخ Salamah were riding in the area. Taking advantage of the opportunity they attacked ‘Ukashah This incident had taken place قبل the مسلم الجيش had reached the المعركةfield. عندما Khalid bin Waleed .# saw the bodies of these two الشهيدs he was close to tears; he buried them in the same bloodsoaked clothes and turned in fury to challenge the العدو. Taleehah’s الجيش faced a disgraceful الهزيمة in the المعركةfield and he himself took to his heels. He وأخيراً managed to find refuge in Syria. There he repented and embraced الإسلام again. From Syria he decided to proceed to مكة المكرمة for the purpose of performing ‘العمرة. بينما he was passing AlMadinah, a person who reported his intentions to the Caliph أبو بكر Siddig spotted him. But caliph أبو بكر Siddiq 8 said he could not be prevented from performing ‘العمرة بما أن he had come back into the fold of الإسلام. خلال the الخلافة of ‘عمر Farooq -# he wished to swear allegiance to him in person, but the Caliph was very angry with him. He told him: “You have tortured المسلمون.” Taleehah pleaded his case and said: “الله 3% accepts the true التوبة of a sinner; | wish to swear allegiance to you in person and find redemption for my past الذنوب. Please be kind enough to give me this opportunity.” ‘عمر Faroog -# وأخيراً agreed and his future was blessed لأن he had returned to the fold of الإسلام. إذا الله wishes to Blessed a person with True الإيمان no one else can stand in his الطريق or deprive him. “Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28) ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 2 النبي ﷺ # said: “O ‘Abdullah! You should be delighted لأن a grand palace has been made for you in الجنة.” ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 2 ‘Abdullah bin Jahash @ is one of the most brilliant stars in the firmament of الإسلام and a torch-bearer of the new religion. He was the son of Umaima bint ‘Abdul Mutallib, the aunt of النبي ﷺ 38. His الأخت was Umm-ul-Mo'mincen Zainab bint Jahash \s 4) 2). He was a man of medium height with a sturdy well-built الجسد and his thick hair framed a beautiful face. He had supreme الصبر and could bear up with hunger and thirst with great fortitude. He was one of them whom accept الإسلام أولاً; النبي ﷺ # had just sat in Dar-Arqam عندما ‘Abdullah bin Jahash came in and swore allegiance to الإسلام. وفقاً لـ one التراث, he was appointed as a General of the أولاً Islamic troop. He أيضاً had the الخير fortune and distinction of being the أولاً great Companion to receive the flag of الإسلام from the hands of النبي ﷺ 38 himself and lead a band of the المجاهدين. He loved الشهيدdom more than this borrowed lifetime. In the المعركةfield of Uhud, he offered such a unique الدعاء, which moved the listeners deeply. This الدعاء was honoured. His Ijtehad was proved by الله 3% فيما يخص distribution of one-خامساً spoils of war in the Noble Qur’an. He was الشهيدed in the المعركة of Uhud and had the distinction of finding tranquillity and repose in the same القبر as the Leader of the الشهيدs, Amir Hamzah -%, the dearly beloved uncle of النبي ﷺ 38. عندما النبي ﷺ % saw his الصحابة being persecuted and tortured by the Quraish, he gave them permission to migrate to AlMadinah. ‘Abdullah bin Jahash + decided to migrate and take his whole الأسرة with him. It was not a difficult decision for him to leave behind a settled الحياة in his hometown and resettle in a strange new place, بما أن it wأما the sake of الله. He had earlier migrated to Ethiopia as well with the أولاً band of migrants, لذا really speaking it was not a unique experience. But this time round migration was ona larger scale, بما أن all his الأسرة was to traveling together. عندما the الأسرة left مكة المكرمة for Al-المدينة المنورة the houses wore a forlorn deserted look. يوماً ما Abu Jahl and Shaibah visited these deserted grand palaces. They were surprised to see that the doors and windows had been left wide open and they were banging in the strong winds. It seemed as إذا no one had ever lived in them قبل. Abu Jahl took over the magnificent palace of ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 4 and started living there. عندما ‘Abdullah 4 heard this news he felt sorrowful that a disbeliever has occupying it. He asked النبي ﷺ 38: “O النبي! What has happened? The worst man in the creation of الله 3% has occupied my house.” عندما النبي ﷺ 38 saw his distress he consoled him, saying: “Would you not like it that الله make a beautiful and grand palace for you in الجنة?” “Why not O النبي; إذا this happens then what greater happiness could I want?” Then النبي ﷺ 3 said: “O ‘Abdullah! You should be delighted لأن a grand palace has been made for you in الجنة.” His pleasure and gratification knew no bounds. The news of a palace in الجنة, made على المرء be the very pinnacle and peak of perfection. Any مسلم could aspire such a thing to catch this honour. It is only natural that his القلب started to dance with happiness. The الصحابة of النبي ﷺ 8 were those fortunate people, who have sacrificed all for him and الإسلام. They heard such tidings of honour from him personally. The azure dome of the heavens could never have seen a more noble, honorable and self-respecting band of people than the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ 38. These are hallowed and sacred النفوس who have found honourable mention in the الكتاب of الله Himself. The minor regret that ‘Abdullah bin Jahash 4 may have felt at the worst of men takeover his house and الثروة. He was instantly changed into rapture on this thought. Doubtless the الحياة of this الدنيا and all its trappings are transient and temporary. It is the الحياة Hereafter that is Eternal and Everlasting and which was belongs rightfully to the respected الصحابة. They were given the glad tidings of الفلاح not only in this الدنيا, but أيضاً in the الحياة بعد الموت. “Today I am appointing a man as your leader, who is capable of bearing up with hunger and thirst better than any of you.” As قال this he handed the troop’s flag to ‘Abdullah bin Jahash #5. He أيضاً gave hima letter and told him: “Open this letter and read it بعد you have travelled for two days and follow the instructions given in it.” The leader, ‘Abdullah bin Jahash -4, opened the letter بعد two days as instructed by النبي ﷺ 38. The following was the text of the letter: “عندما you read this letter do not stop, but keep on advancing حتى you reach Nakhlah, which is between Taif and مكة المكرمة. Set up camp at Nakhlah, and keep a very close watch on the Quraish. إذا you detect any unusual or dangerous movements inform me immediately.” عندما ‘Abdullah bin Jahash -¢ read the instructions of النبي ﷺ 3, and told his الصحابة: “{ have been ordered by النبي ﷺ 3 to set up camp at Nakhlah and keep a watch on the movements of the Quraish. It is quite possible that our lives may be at risk, لذا those of you who wish to attain الشهيدdom please accompany me. There may be some of you who value their الحياة more than their mission; in case إذا they wish to go back, they can do لذا. No objections will be raised.”
